observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
1. The AMT to submit the disbursement vouchers pertaining to the
abovementioned unrecorded checks issued with its supporting
documents to the MA and strictly adhere to the check disbursement
process provided under Section 44 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
2. The MA to draw Journal Entry Vouchers to record the book
reconciling items to adjust and correct the affected accounts in the
general ledger, pursuant to Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections
3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 to prove the correctness and
existence of the cash in bank balances as of year-end.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the AMT and
the MA to reconcile the CB and journal balances periodically, specifically per quarter,
and accordingly effect the adjustment in order to arrive at an accurate and reconciled
balance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the:
1. AMT and the MA to properly communicate and periodically reconcile the
Cashbook-Cash Advance and journal balances to avoid accumulated errors
and unreconciled balances; and
2. MA to prepare the necessary entries in the books to adjust/correct the balance
of the Advances for Payroll account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
1. Review and update the E.O. No. 07 s. 2022 to ensure that the frequency of
inventory counts and the submission of related reports are clearly
established. These procedures should align with the NGAS Manual
requirements, specifically mandating semi-annual physical inventory
counts, with RPCIs submitted to the Auditor by July 31 and January 31 of
each year;
2. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to:
a. Immediately identify and locate the missing items and submit to the
Audit Team a revised RPCI as of December 31, 2024 to support the
Inventory account balance as of year-end; and
b. Actively oversee the inventory process, ensuring that the inventory
count and related reports are certified by the committee. This will
ensure accuracy and integrity in the reporting process, making the
inventory reports reliable and transparent;
3. Require the MA to:
a. Draw the appropriate adjusting entries, based on valid supporting
documents, to exclude the distributed relief goods from the balance
of Inventory Accounts to reflect its accurate balance;
b. Exert all efforts to obtain information regarding the non-existent
inventories recorded in the account “Other Supplies and Materials
Inventory”. If no data is obtained, after all efforts were made, follow
the procedures laid out in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for disposition
of non-existent/missing assets; and
c. Henceforth, record all purchases of supplies and materials for stock
as Inventories, and recognize expense when the inventories are sold,
exchanged or distributed, pursuant to Section 114, Chapter 1 of the
Manual on the NGAs for LGUs and paragraph 44 of the IPSAS 12.
4. Require the GSO Designate to:
a. Submit to the MA the duly accomplished and verified distribution
sheets for adjustment to expense of the distributed portion of the
inventories under the account “Welfare Goods for Distribution”, in
coordination with the MSWDO;
b. Prepare the SSMIs weekly, supported with RIS for supplies and
materials issued and the MA to prepare JEV as supported by SSMIs
and RIS submitted by the GSO to record issuance of supplies and
materials; and
c. Prepare and maintain stock cards for each inventory item, even in
the absence of a stock room, to track the movement of the inventories,
including both receipts and issuances, hence, ensuring that no item is
overlooked and that the balance on hand can be easily determined at a
given time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MA to:
1. Conduct a detailed review and analysis of the “Due to BIR” account to come
up with the correct unremitted balances to ensure fair presentation in the
financial statements. Thereafter, prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if
warranted;
2. Upon reconciliation, remit immediately the determined unpaid obligations
to the concerned government agency to avoid incurrence of penalties and
surcharges for delay or non-remittance; and
3. Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of the account balance and
closely monitor the preparation of the needed schedules for the remittance
of the withheld/deducted amounts and ensure its remittance in full within
the prescribed deadlines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the MA to
coordinate with the OIC-Municipal Engineer to locate the necessary documentation
for dormant items in the CIP account. If documentation is unavailable, the OIC-
Municipal Engineer should conduct a site inspection to verify the existence and
status of the projects. For completed assets, the OIC-Municipal Engineer provide supporting
documentation (i.e. project completion certifications, inspection and acceptance
reports, other relevant reports) to confirm their completion and readiness for use.

We also recommended that the LCE direct the MA to:
1. Promptly reclassify completed assets from CIP to the appropriate PPE
account, if documentation confirms project completion, and ensure
depreciation is recorded starting the period following the asset’s availability
for use, in compliance with PPSAS 17; and
2. Review the remaining balances of CIP accounts to ensure that all amounts
are accurately recorded in accordance with the provisions of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, and, if necessary, make the appropriate adjustments to
reflect the correct balances.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the:
a) Inventory Committee (IC) to conduct physical count of the inventories and
reconcile the same with the Accounting and GSO records; and
b) General Services Officer to prepare and submit the SSMIs to the
Accounting Office to aid in the reconciliation and adjustment of the
inventory accounts.

We recommended that the LCE:
a) Instruct the AMT to submit the remaining eight DVs together with its SDs
to the MAO and strictly adhere with the check disbursement process
provided under Section 44 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I; and
b) Direct the MA to record in the book of accounts the unrecorded
disbursements upon submission of the DVs and its SDs by the MT in order
to reflect the accurate balances of cash, equity and expense accounts of the
LGU in the financial statements; and thereafter, submit the same to the
Office of the Auditor for audit.

We recommended that the LCE:
1. Require the concerned AOs to liquidate their CAs within the reglementary
period and refund any excess CAs for timely recognition in the books and
reporting of the financial transactions and to minimize the risk of loss or
misuse;
2. Direct the MA to defer the grant of additional cash advances unless settlement of
previous advances were made;
3. Require the MA to send notices to concerned personnel after the lapse of
the reglementary period to settle their accounts. Otherwise, administrative
and legal sanctions for non-compliance shall be imposed to persons liable; and
4. Strictly enforce the laws, rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and
liquidation of CAs as provided for under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA
Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the:
1. Disposal Committee to conduct the immediate disposal of the unserviceable
properties pursuant to the procedures set by Section 79 of PD No. 1445; and
2. After disposal, the MA to record the derecognition of the unserviceable
properties from the books of account.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant (MA) to:
1. Reconcile the balances of the accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs
with the counterpart accounts of the Province, and of other LGUs
(barangays) as warranted; and
2. Ensure that timely and accurate recording of all inter-agency transactions
are practiced to avoid the same occurrence in the future.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant (MA) to:
1. Identify and investigate the discrepancies, and once established, make the
necessary adjustments to reconcile account balances as of the reporting
period; and
2. Practice regular review and reconciliation to minimize or avoid
discrepancies of the accounts’ balances.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to coordinate with the source
agency and reconcile the account balance presented in the LGU’s books with BFAR
Region V to resolve/settle the differences in their books.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Office of the Social Service Division of the LGU as frontline office, to find out the status of each loan recipients as to their existence and whereabouts. Afterwards, instruct the MA to send demand letter immediately to all erring borrowers to inform them that the amounts granted to them were not dole-outs from the government and prompt them to pay their overdue obligations.
observations.managementAction: The management will send demand letters.
observations.reason: The MA was not yet employed when these loans were granted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit justification for the delayed completion of projects as well as approval of requests for time extensions without valid reasons. In addition, we recommended the immediate submission of other documents pertaining to the remaining six projects.
observations.managementAction: The management will verify the projects to be able to submit the required justification and other documents.
observations.reason: No justification submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) directs the Municipal Accountant (MA) to ensure that recorded PPEs are only those items of assets that meets the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00. We also recommend strict compliance with Section 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
require the MA and GSO-Designate to reconcile the discrepancy between the PPE accounts recorded in the books with that appearing in the RPCPPE and effect the necessary adjustment/s to come up with the correct balances; and
direct the GSO to reconcile the results of inventory with the property records on file together with the Acknowledgement Receipts of Equipment and Inventory Custodian Slips issued to end users using the RPCPPE as basis and discrepancies should be verified and adjusted/corrected accordingly.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the:
GSO–Designate to institute measures to dispose properly all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the procedures set by Section 79 of PD No. 1445; and
Municipal Accountant (MA) to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements (FS) all unserviceable properties that were not yet disposed as of December 31, 2020 to present fairly the PPE in the FS.
observations.managementAction: Management will compose the Disposal Committee.
observations.reason: No Disposal        Commitee

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:
require the MA to prepare immediately the JEV cancelling the remaining 15 stale checks amounting to ₱55,535 and adjust the books accordingly; and
accordingly, require the AMT to send notice to the payees of existence of checks whether unissued or not yet encashed at least one month before these become stale.

We recommended that the LCE:
require the concerned officials and employees to liquidate immediately the outstanding balances of their CAs;
stop the granting of additional CAs to accountable officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; and
stop granting CA to unauthorized officers and employees; and
enforce to all personnel strict compliance with Sections 3.2, 4.1.2, 4.1.3 and 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction:
Management will resend demand letters and furnish copies to the Audit Team.
Management adopted a new scheme to minimize unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason:
Concerned officials
and employees did
not settle their
accounts despite
receipt of demand
letters.
Laxity in the
enforcement of the
guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE instruct the Municipal DRRM Officer to utilize the LDRRMF strictly for implementation of projects and activities exclusively for disaster prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery as envisioned in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01.

We recommended that the LCE instruct:
the MDRRMC to plan, identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to make the Investment Plan more comprehensive and integrated for easy monitoring of project implementation and fund utilization; and
the MA to disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to FS.
observations.managementAction: For compliance this CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the MPDO to:
identify and prioritize the development projects that could be viably implemented and to be funded out of the 20% DF; and
submit to the AT the Accomplishment Report vis-à-vis 20% Development Fund Plan indicating the status of their implementation; and
Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation and completion of developmental PPAs for the benefit of the LGU’s constituents.
observations.managementAction:
Audit Observation was reiterated under Observation No. 8.
Audit Observation was reiterated under Observation No. 8.
Audit Observation was reiterated under Observation No. 8.
observations.reason:
.
Delayed preparation
Insufficient funding, road right of way validation, technical assistance of geodetic engineer hindered the implementation and completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to submit proof of compliance with the requirement on price negotiation in accordance with Section 5 of the GPPB Circular 01-2020.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will submit the necessary documents.
observations.reason:
The BAC did not
submit proof of
compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the MSWDO to:
facilitate the complete filling-up of the SAC forms of all paid beneficiaries; 
re-determine/identify the ineligible beneficiaries and apply to them the appropriate course of action cited under Section VIII-B(6.1) of DSWD MC No. 09; and
ensure strict compliance with DSWD-DOLE-DTI-DA-DOF-DBM-DILG JMC No. 1 and DSWD MC No. 09, both Series of 2020.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO is still facilitating the processing of the filling up of the forms and will furnish copies to the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
The MSWDO did not
furnish the Audit
Team of the
completed and
filled-up SAC forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the AMT and MA to comply strictly with Item 2.6(a), Chapter 2, Part II of the BOM for LGUs in the determination of funds available for supplemental budgets.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to remit to the BIR, GSIS and Pag-IBIG the total amount of ₱156,675 and to comply strictly with Section 2.58 of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018, Section 114C, NIRC of 1997, Section 6(b) and Section 3.6 Rule II, of RA No. 8291 and RA No. 7742 and Section 3(a), Rule VII of RA No. 9679.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to submit immediately to the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) all paid DVs, payrolls, official receipts. Subsequently, require the MA to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim financial statements together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity to the abovementioned provisions.
observations.reason:
Delayed submission
of reports of
accountable officers,
unclaimed checks of
suppliers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Strictly enforce the settlement of all unliquidated CAs as of December 31, 2019; 
Stop the granting of additional CAs to accountable officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; and
Stop the granting of additional CAs to accountable officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same.
observations.reason:
Despite demand letters sent to concerned officials and employees, only few settled their CAs.
Non-observance of the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the approval of CLUP and revisit its CDP and ensure that climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction activities will be integrated in its land use plan.
observations.managementAction: The LGU finished crafting the CLUP and will be subjected for public hearing.  Further, updating of the CDP is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE coordinate immediately with the Sangguniang Bayan to act favorably on the submitted MDC Resolution that will expedite the implementation of the four targeted projects for CY 2019.
We also recommended that the 20% DF is optimally utilized to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. Specifically, we suggested that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to include only projects chargeable to 20% DF that are most viable and can be physically implemented during the fiscal year without any impediment. Also, require the MPDO to submit monitoring report on the status of implementation of PPAs and use the same as basis in making decision in project implementation strictly in accordance with the approved timetable so that benefits therefrom could be realized the earliest possible time; and Municipal Engineer’s Office to fast track the implementation and completion of developmental PPAs for the benefit of the LGU’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: Audit Observation was reiterated under Observation No. 8.
observations.reason: Projects are included in the continuing appropriations like the drainage system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the MT and the MA to reconcile immediately their records and effect the necessary adjustments to come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Treasury account. Henceforth, reconcile regularly the GL and the cashbook so that reconciling items could be properly and timely recorded.
observations.managementAction: The MA is still in the process of reconciling the difference for the GF.
observations.reason: The Cash in Treasury account did not tally with the cash book balance as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE intensify follow ups to all barangays to submit immediately their liquidation and utilization reports in order to take up the corresponding expenditures in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The MA will again follow up the Barangays to submit the liquidation reports immediately.
observations.reason: Several barangays were not able to liquidate the funds in 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the MA to expedite coordination with the Provincial Accountant and verify the discrepancies noted in the account balances of Due to LGUs account and prepare corresponding adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.reason: No breakdown of the unreconciled balances between the LGU and the province were provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to: Plan thoroughly any project identifying major items crucial to construction that should be prioritized and included in the Project Plan and Program of Work; and Submit immediately to the Audit Team (AT) copy of the subject project contract together with all documents forming part thereof. Henceforth, all infrastructure contracts and supporting documents shall be submitted to the COA within five days from perfection thereof as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001. We also recommended that Management ensure that written agreement by way of Deed of Donation/Sale/Usufruct is entered into by the LGU and private individual whenever the LGU will be constructing structures in private lots. Also, similar contract shall be perfected when the LGU allows rental of government properties to private party.  A Municipal Ordinance shall be enacted prescribing the rates of rental of government properties.

We recommended that Management: Require the HRMO together with the concerned DHs or create a committee that would formulate standard policy guidelines to strengthen internal controls on hiring/rehiring of JOs with the end in view of: limiting the number of hired/rehired personnel based on actual manpower need by each office; assuring that JOs do not perform functions of existing regular employees; declaring that there will be no duplication of works among JOs assigned in one Office; and As preparatory procedure, we suggest that the agency review their functions, systems and procedures, organizational structure and staffing to determine the appropriate manpower complement for their programs/activities/projects as provided in Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO 292 due to absence of hiring and rehiring guidelines.

We commended Management for submitting the RPCPPE for CY 2019.  We also recommended that the agency keep up the commendable feat of coming up with a reconciled balance of PPE in the financial statements and RPCPPE.

We recommended that the LCE require the MDRRM Officer to plan properly all PPAs funded from 70% Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events or conditions.

We commended Management for the effective implementation of the 20% DF in CY 2018. However, we still recommended that the LCE direct the officials concerned to ensure that 100 percent completion and implementation of PPAs under the 20% of IRA be attained to achieve the desirable socio-economic development.

We recommended that the LCE require the MDRRM Officer to plan properly all programs/projects/activities funded from 70% Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events or conditions.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to expedite coordination with the Provincial Accountant and verify the discrepancies noted in the account balances of Due to LGUs account and prepare corresponding adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.reason: No breakdown of the unreconciled balances between the LGU and the province were provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require all employees’ loan applications to government and private financial institutions shall be submitted first to the MA who will note in the loan application the present net take home pay of the loan applicant. We recommended that the LCE disapprove loan application of all employees with net take home pay below ₱5,000. Henceforth, we recommended that the MA shall monitor the take home pay employees and the payroll preparer shall observe strictly that employees shall receive the mandatory ₱5,000 net take home pays in compliance with Section 48 of the General Provisions of GAA of FY 2018.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is monitoring the loans of employees to ensure that required net take home pay is followed.
observations.reason: Payroll still reflects net take home pay of ₱5,000 because employees still had outstanding loans from lending institutions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality follow-up the approval of the Closure and Rehabilitation Plan from DENR-EMB Region V and seek immediately the Authority to Close the controlled dumpsite. Henceforth, cause the closure of the LGU’s existing CDF and facilitate the immediate construction of a proper sanitary landfill. Such immediate action is necessary to address if not prevent, detrimental ecological issues and their effects on human health and to the environment.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MT to submit immediately to the MAO all paid DVs, payrolls, official receipts. Subsequently, require the MA to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim FS together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity to the above-mentioned provisions.
observations.reason: Some of the vouchers were not submitted to the MAO on time hence, recording and submission to COA was delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE secure legal documents as proof of ownership of the land where the agency structures were built. We also recommended that the LCE ensure that ownership of lands is legally transferred to the LGU to avoid future legal actions from rightful owners.
We recommended that the LCE require the MPDO as well as the GFPS Technical Working Group to facilitate the establishment of GAD Database.
observations.reason: No reason provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Supply Officer-Designate to: Facilitate the tagging of all assets of the LGU; Ensure that property numbers in the RPCPPE are the same number in the property tags and ARE; and Tag PPE showing the complete/required information as above enumerated. We also recommended that the Supply Officer Designate ensure that no PPE are distributed to its end-user without the property tags to ensure completeness of information in the property tags such as tag inventory number, property number, serial number of the property, description and other salient information.
observations.managementAction: The management will start the tagging of properties this CY 2022 and assured that it will be completed on or before the end of CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
require the MA and GSO-Designate to reconcile the discrepancy between the PPE accounts recorded in the books with that appearing in the RPCPPE and effect the necessary adjustment/s to come up with the correct balances; and
direct the GSO to reconcile the results of inventory with the property records on file together with the Acknowledgement Receipts of Equipment and Inventory Custodian Slips issued to end users using the RPCPPE as basis and discrepancies should be verified and adjusted/corrected accordingly.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the:
GSO–Designate to institute measures to dispose properly all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the procedures set by Section 79 of PD No. 1445; and
Municipal Accountant (MA) to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements (FS) all unserviceable properties that were not yet disposed as of December 31, 2020 to present fairly the PPE in the FS.

We recommended that the MAO provide depreciation for all depreciable PPEs and to record adjustment both to the Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity accounts.
We also recommended strict compliance with PPSAS 17 and Section 2.1 of COA Circular Letter No. 2004-003.

We recommended that the LCE:
require the MA to prepare immediately the JEV cancelling the remaining 15 stale checks amounting to ₱55,535 and adjust the books accordingly; and
accordingly, require the AMT to send notice to the payees of existence of checks whether unissued or not yet encashed at least one month before these become stale.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal DRRM Officer to utilize the LDRRMF strictly for implementation of projects and activities exclusively for disaster prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery as envisioned in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01.

We recommended that the LCE instruct:
the MDRRMC to plan, identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to make the Investment Plan more comprehensive and integrated for easy monitoring of project implementation and fund utilization; and
the MA to disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to FS.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the:
MPDO to:
identify and prioritize the development projects that could be viably implemented during planning of PPAs to be charged from the 20% DF to maximize its utilization; and
submit to the AT the Accomplishment Report vis-à-vis 20% DF Plan indicating the status of their implementation; and
Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation and completion of developmental PPAs for the benefit of the LGU’s constituents.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to submit proof of compliance with the requirement on price negotiation in accordance with Section 5 of the GPPB Circular 01-2020.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MSWDO to:
facilitate the complete filling-up of the SAC forms of all paid beneficiaries;
re-determine/identify the ineligible beneficiaries and apply to them the appropriate course of action cited under Section VIII-B(6.1) of DSWD MC No. 09; and
ensure strict compliance with DSWD-DOLE-DTI-DA-DOF-DBM-DILG JMC No. 1 and DSWD MC No. 09, both Series of 2020.
We recommended that the LCE require the AMT and MA to comply strictly with Item 2.6(a), Chapter 2, Part II of the BOM for LGUs in the determination of funds available for supplemental budgets.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to remit to the BIR, GSIS and Pag-IBIG the total amount of ₱156,675 and to comply strictly with Section 2.58 of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018, Section 114C, NIRC of 1997, Section 6(b) and Section 3.6 Rule II, of RA No. 8291,and  RA No. 7742 and Section 3(a), Rule VII of  RA No. 9679.

We recommended that management submit justification/reason for its inability to submit perfected contracts to the Office of the Auditor on time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately copies of the contracts and corresponding supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. We also recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 on the timely submission of contracts.
observations.managementAction:
For CY 2019, Contracts were not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

The management did not take action on the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the concerned LGU officials to furnish the COA with copies of perfected contracts within the prescribed period for timely evaluation and review.
observations.reason:
Contracts not
submitted to the
Office of the
Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management  require the Supply Officer-Designate to:

Facilitate the tagging of all assets of the LGU;

Ensure that property numbers in the RPCPPE are the same number in the property tags and ARE; and

c. Tag PPE showing the complete/required information as above enumerated.

We also recommended that the Supply Officer Designate ensure that no PPE are distributed to its end-user without the property tags to ensure completeness of information in the property tags such as, tag inventory number, property number, serial number of the property, description and other salient information.
observations.managementAction: Management has yet to take action on the recommendations
observations.reason:
Manpower Constraint
(no permanent
supply officer)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile immediately their records and effect the necessary adjustments to come up with the correct balance of the Cash in Treasury account.  Henceforth, reconcile regularly the GL and the cashbook so that reconciling items could be properly and timely recorded.

We recommended that Management:
enforce strictly the settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2019; and
stop granting additional CAs to accountable officers, officials and employees unless previous CAs are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Office of the Social Service Division of the LGU as frontline office, to find out the status of each loan recipients as to their existence and whereabouts.  Afterwards, instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters immediately to all erring borrowers to inform them that the amounts granted to them were not dole-outs from the government and prompt them to pay their overdue obligations.

We recommended that the LCE intensify follow ups to all recipient barangays to submit immediately their liquidation and utilization reports in order to take up the corresponding expenditures in the books of the LGU.
Also, we further recommended that no additional fund transfers/assistance be released by the LGU to all barangays unless previous fund transfers/assistance are already liquidated.

We commended Management for submitting the RPCPPE for CY 2019 which was reconciled with total PPE balance as of December 31, 2019.   We also recommended that the agency keep up the commendable feat of coming up with a reconciled balance of PPE in the financial statements and RPCPPE.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify immediately the current portion of the long-term loan payable amounting to ₱3.60 million to Loans Payable – Current, Domestic but under the Current Liabilities portion in the Statement of Financial Position.
We recommended that LCE require the Head of Accounting Office to ensure prompt release of the RPT shares of the province within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to expedite coordination with the Provincial Accountant and verify the discrepancies noted in the account balances of Due to LGUs account and prepare corresponding adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal DRRM Officer to plan properly all PPAs funded from 70% Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events or conditions.

We recommended that management prioritize the approval of the CLUP and revisit its CDP and ensure that climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction activities will be integrated in its land use plan.

We commended the agency’s compliance with previous audit recommendation of facilitating the closure of the dumpsite.  However, we recommended that the Municipality cause the immediate construction of a temporary containment building and other required structures to ensure that residual wastes are properly handled and would not contaminate the ground.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the attachments as required under Section 4.1.C.8.5 of the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No, 2013-1 and provide sufficient details of the activities undertaken to facilitate validation of the reported GAD programs and projects.

We recommended that the LCE to direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately to the Accounting Office all paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, and official receipts.  Subsequently, require the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim financial statements together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity to the above mentioned provisions.

We recommended that Management:
Require the HRM Officer together with concerned DHs or create a committee that would formulate standard policy guidelines to strengthen internal controls on hiring/rehiring of JOs with the end in view of:
a.1	limiting the number of hired/rehired JO workers based on actual manpower need by each office;
a.2	assuring that JO workers do not perform functions of existing regular employees; and
a.3	declaring that there will be no duplication of works among JO workers assigned in one Office.
As preparatory procedure, we suggested that the agency review their functions, systems and procedures, organizational structure and staffing to determine the appropriate manpower complement for their PPAs as provided in Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO 292 due to absence of hiring and rehiring guidelines.
Require the JO workers to submit Accomplishment Reports detailing actual works done for the month.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to:
Plan thoroughly any project identifying major items crucial to construction that should be prioritized and included in the Project Plan and Program of Work; and
Submit immediately to the Audit Team copy of the subject project contract together with all documents forming part thereof.  Henceforth, all infrastructure contracts and supporting documents shall be submitted to the COA within five days from perfection thereof as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001.
We also recommended that Management ensure that written agreement by way of Deed of Donation/Sale/Usufruct is entered into by the LGU and private individual whenever the LGU will be constructing structures in private lots.  Also, similar contract shall be perfected when the LGU allows rental of government properties to private party.  A Municipal Ordinance shall be enacted prescribing the rates of rental of government properties.

We recommended that the LCE coordinate immediately with the Sangguniang Bayan to act favourably on the submitted MDC Resolution that will expedite the implementation of the four targeted projects for CY 2019.
We also recommended that the 20% DF is optimally utilized to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.  Specifically, we suggested that the LCE direct the:
Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to include only projects chargeable to 20% DF that are most viable and can be physically implemented during the fiscal year without any impediment.  Also, require the MPDO to submit monitoring report on the status of implementation of PPAs and use the same as basis in making decision in project implementation strictly in accordance with the approved timetable so that benefits therefrom could be realized the earliest possible time; and
Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of projects that should have been completed in CY 2019.

We recommended that the agency keep up the efficient revenue collection. We also recommended that:
The Local Finance Committee:
Conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue, taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets;
Conduct periodic review and monitoring of actual performance so that appropriate measures could be undertaken to achieve targets and further improve collection efficiency; and
Provide targets for accounts without projections based on statistics, prior years’ experience and future expectations.
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office and other departments involved in generating revenues to intensify further collection of revenues that missed the targets.

We recommended that the LCE direct: 
a) the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of PPE and render report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual. The progress of the inventory taking should be monitored by the Local Chief Executive; and

b) the designated GSO personnel as well the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the PPE balances between their records as required under Section 13, Volume II of the NGAS Manual and discrepancies should be verified and adjusted.

We commended Management for the effective implementation of the 20% EDF in CY 2018.  We still recommended that the LCE direct the officials concerned to ensure that 100 percent completion and implementation of PPAs under the 20% of IRA be attained to achieve the desirable socio economic development.
observations.managementAction: The management had lapses in project execution.
observations.reason:
The LGU’s implementation reached only 53% in
CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:

a)  Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and

b) Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

We commended Management for the effective implementation of GAD PPAs in CY 2018.  We also recommended that the agency through its LCE prioritize full implementation of all GAD PPAs in succeeding years.

We recommended that Management submit justification for the delayed completion of projects as well as approval of requests for time extensions without valid reasons.  In addition, we recommended the immediate submission of other documents pertaining to the remaining six projects.

We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to monitor strictly the implementation of infrastructure projects to meet the stipulated completion time.  Also, refrain from approving request for time extensions without valid reasons.

No justification
submitted to the
Office of the
Auditor.

Some projects
implemented in CY
2019 were still
delayed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management submit justification/reason for its inability to submit variation orders to the Office of the Auditor on time.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately copies of the variation orders and corresponding supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. We also recommend that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 on the timely submission of contracts and variation orders.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to return immediately the unexpended fund transfers including interest income accruing thereto to the source agency as mandated by the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare immediately the JEV cancelling the 33 stale checks amounting to ₱245,218 and adjust the books based on proposed adjusting journal entry.

We recommended that the LCE require the MDRRM Officer to plan properly all programs/projects/activities funded from 70% Mitigation Fund to make these feasible for implementation during the year to anticipate, respond to and recover effectively from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazardous events or conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Local DRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the required report and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned LGU Officials and employees to submit written explanations why they charged their claims for registration fees, travelling expenses and per diems against public funds despite the fact that pursuing a master’s degree was considered personal in nature and that there was no appropriation for this purpose.

Henceforth, we recommended that the LCE direct the concerned personnel to stop immediately charging all expenses arising from any pursuit of master’s degree and the Municipal Accountant to reject any of the said claims.

The subject disallowance is under
appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to expedite coordination with the Provincial Accountant and verify the discrepancies noted in the account balances of Due to LGUs account and prepare corresponding adjusting entries, if necessary.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require all employees’ loan applications to government and private financial institutions shall be submitted first to the Municipal Accountant who will note in the loan application the present net take home pay of the loan applicant.  We recommended that the LCE disapprove loan application of all employees with net take home pay below ₱5,000.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall monitor the take home pay of employees and the payroll preparer shall observe strictly that employees shall receive the mandatory ₱5,000 net take home pays in compliance with Section 48 of the General Provisions of GAA of FY 2018

employees still had
outstanding loans
from lending
institutions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality follow-up the approval of the Closure and Rehabilitation Plan from DENR-EMB Region V and seek immediately the Authority to Close the controlled dumpsite.  Such action is necessary to address unauthorized closure and facilitate the proper implementation of the Closure Plan.  Henceforth, cause the closure of the LGU’s existing CDF and facilitate the immediate construction of a proper sanitary landfill.  Such immediate action is necessary to address if not prevent, detrimental ecological issues and their effects on human health and to the environment.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately copies of the contracts and corresponding supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. We also recommend that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 on the timely submission of contracts.

Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and
Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

Henceforth, we recommended that the LCE direct the concerned personnel to stop immediately charging all expenses arising from any pursuit of master’s degree and the Municipal Accountant to reject any of the said claims.

We recommended that the LCE require all employees’ loan applications to government and private financial institutions to be submitted first to the Municipal Accountant who will note in the loan application the present net take home pay of the loan applicant. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall monitor the take home pay of employees and the payroll preparer shall observe strictly that employees shall receive the mandatory ₱5,000 net take home pays in compliance with Section 48 of the General Provisions of GAA of FY 2018.

Henceforth, cause the closure of the LGU’s existing CDF and facilitate the immediate construction of a proper sanitary landfill.  Such immediate action is necessary to address if not prevent, detrimental ecological issues and their effects on human health and to the environment.

Subsequently, require the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim financial statements together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity to the above mentioned provisions.

We recommended that the LCE direct the LCPC officials to ensure that appropriation for the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) out of the 1 percent of IRA be made and likewise utilized for the prompt implementation of the programs and projects in order to optimally achieve the desirable impact of addressing the development and welfare of children and youth as prescribed under DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended the Municipal SWM Board, with the Municipal Health Officer and other concerned agencies, provide necessary training as well as protective equipment and paraphernalia to SWM staff to ensure that solid wastes are properly handled and the risk of hazards to the SWM staff and the public are avoided or minimized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure that the composition of the Municipal Waste Management Board complies with Section 5 of the IRR of RA No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure effective formulation and implementation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended for the agency that GAD planning and budgeting shall be observed annually and be incorporated in all programming and budgeting exercise of the LGU. Also, at least five percent of the total agency budget appropriations as authorized, should be the cost of implementing the GAD activities as part of their approved budget.
We recommended that the LCE secure legal documents as proof of ownership of the land where the agency structures were built.
